meta.sprintTitle: Client Portal v1 - Per-Page Layout Picker + Safe Content Remapping
meta.goal: Allow end users to change the look of an individual page by selecting from compatible layouts in the client portal while preserving mapped text and image content safely.

whyNow: Users eventually need the ability to vary page appearance without editing raw coordinates
Canonical layouts and versioning now make page-level switching feasible and safer
This delivers a high-value customization feature while staying within controlled layout options
productContext: The user experience should feel like choosing a page look, not building a page from scratch
Per-page layout switching should preserve chapter/story content and images where possible
Only compatible layouts should be offered to avoid broken mapping and export regressions

nonGoals: Arbitrary movement of slots by end users
Automatic migration across incompatible layout families
Theme designer or global style system
dependencies: Stable slot metadata on populated frames

operational: User can change a page to another compatible layout from the client portal
Switching a page layout preserves mapped text and image content where a compatible slot exists
Incompatible layout options are filtered or clearly warned
product: Users can vary page appearance without learning drag-and-drop layout editing
Page customization feels controlled and elder-friendly
Layout switching increases perceived flexibility without sacrificing export reliability
quality: Switching layouts updates pageLayoutId and resulting frames deterministically
Remap rules are predictable and reversible within undo/history expectations
Exports reflect the newly selected layout without manual repair in common cases
security: Users can only select layouts exposed to their template/version and compatibility context
Server validates layout switch requests and remapping eligibility
Layout switching does not expose hidden/internal template layouts

personas.name: Studio User
personas.description: Wants to change the look of a single page without rebuilding it.

personas.name: Elder/Low-confidence User
personas.description: Needs simple, safe page customization options.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: Needs to understand which layout a user selected for a page.

inScope: Per-page change-layout action in client portal/Studio
Server-side remap of page content from old slots to new slots
Preservation of mapped text and image assets where compatible
Warnings for unmapped or partially mapped content
outOfScope: Drag-and-drop layout editing by end users
Automatic global restyling of all pages in a book

architecture.recommendedApproach: Treat page layout switching as selecting a different compatible PageLayout for a page instance. The system compares old and new slot sets, remaps known content by slot role/kind and explicit compatibility hints, updates pageLayoutId, and regenerates or patches the page frames accordingly.
principles: Offer a curated set of compatible layouts instead of open-ended editing
Preserve user content first, aesthetics second
Only switch when remap confidence is acceptable or user explicitly confirms risks

functionalRequirements.title: Change layout action
requirements: Users can trigger a page-level change layout action from the page controls
The action opens a picker showing only compatible layouts

functionalRequirements.title: Layout picker
requirements: Display layout preview cards with name, short description, and thumbnail or schematic preview
Allow filtering or grouping by page role where helpful
Hide internal-only layouts from normal users

functionalRequirements.title: Safe remapping
requirements: Map text and image content from old slots to compatible new slots using stable slot metadata and compatibility rules
Preserve image asset references and crop where feasible
Preserve rich text content and plain text where feasible

functionalRequirements.title: Warnings and confirmations
requirements: Warn when some content cannot be mapped automatically
Require confirmation for switches with partial mapping or potential overflow risk
Allow cancellation without mutating the page

functionalRequirements.title: Persistence and export compatibility
requirements: Successful layout switch updates pageLayoutId and resulting frames persist
Studio preview and export reflect the new layout
Page layout provenance remains visible to support/admin tooling

recommendedEndpoints.purpose: Return compatible layout options for a page
Switch page to selected compatible layout and remap content

pages.title: Change Layout
pages.purpose: Let users swap the current page to another compatible layout

uxRules: Keep the flow simple: pick page, preview options, confirm
Prefer visual previews over technical slot terminology where possible
Do not offer clearly incompatible layouts
Explain what will happen to existing content before applying a risky switch

technicalTasks.title: Define page layout compatibility rules
technicalTasks.description: Implement logic to decide which layouts are selectable for a given page instance.

technicalTasks.title: Implement remap engine
technicalTasks.description: Map content from old slots to new slots and regenerate page frames safely.

technicalTasks.title: Add page layout picker UI
technicalTasks.description: Create page-level layout change action and picker modal in the client portal/Studio.

technicalTasks.title: Persist layout switches and provenance
technicalTasks.description: Update page references, frame metadata, and project provenance when a layout changes.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add coverage for compatibility filtering, remap outcomes, and UI flows.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user on a page with a compatible alternative layout
acceptanceCriteria.then: they see only compatible layout options with previews

acceptanceCriteria.given: a page with text and image content
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user switches to a compatible layout
acceptanceCriteria.then: mapped text and images are preserved in the new layout where compatible slots exist

acceptanceCriteria.given: a selected layout with partial slot compatibility
acceptanceCriteria.then: the system shows warnings and requires confirmation before applying

acceptanceCriteria.when: the user previews or exports the storybook
acceptanceCriteria.then: the new layout is reflected consistently

risks.risk: Remap rules fail for edge-case pages
risks.mitigation: Use compatibility gating and warning-based confirmation rather than forcing all switches

risks.risk: Users expect full freeform editing after seeing layout switching
risks.mitigation: Frame the feature as changing page look from curated options, not manual design editing

risks.risk: Too many layout options increase cognitive load
risks.mitigation: Curate options per page role and show only compatible/high-quality layouts

definitionOfDone: Users can open a per-page compatible layout picker
Compatible layouts can be selected and applied safely
Page content is remapped where possible and warnings are shown when not
Studio preview and export reflect the new layout
Automated tests cover compatibility, remap, and UI flows
